setting.note: Kawabata's snow country is a hot-spring town on the west side of Honshu, reached from Tokyo by a train that runs south to north through a long mountain tunnel and comes out into deep Niigata snow. The map draws on the real onsen town of Yuzawa: the station, the bathhouses and inns on the slope, the cedars of the shrine, and the border tunnel that opens the book.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Yasunari Kawabata, serialised from 1935 and completed as a novel in 1948; the standard English text is Edward Seidensticker's translation. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Snow Country, Yasunari Kawabata (1948). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cold blue of dusk on snow, the light the train carries into the border country. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
